cases.name: no_event
steps: open a long-poll connection with client_max_time = poll_timeout + 5
publish no event during the window
wait for the poll window to elapse
assert the connection returns 204 within the window
assert the response body is empty

cases.name: event
steps: open a long-poll connection with client_max_time = poll_timeout + 5
trigger one event mid-poll
assert the connection returns 200 within two seconds of the trigger
assert the delivered payload carries the expected event_type

cases.name: multiple_events
steps: open a long-poll connection with client_max_time = poll_timeout + 5
trigger two events during one window
assert both events are delivered
assert the events are queued and none is dropped

cases.name: resume_after_gap
steps: complete one poll and record the documented cursor / Last-Event-ID
publish an event in the gap between polls
open a new poll supplying the recorded cursor / Last-Event-ID
assert the gap event is delivered and not lost

cases.name: concurrent_pollers
steps: open two concurrent poll connections with client_max_time = poll_timeout + 5
publish a broadcast event
assert both clients receive it if broadcast is documented, or exactly one receives it per the documented single-consumer rule
assert behaviour matches the documented policy

cases.name: connection_drop
steps: open a poll connection and disconnect the client mid-poll
assert the dropped client frees its slot
open a fresh poll on the same channel
assert the channel is not wedged and continues to serve new pollers
